messages.content:
Displaying negative, irrational opinions and attitudes toward members of diverse populations is considered to be ________.
Question 1 Select one:

A.
prejudice

B.
discrimination

C.
illegal

D.
immoral

E.
unethical
A. prejudice

Displaying negative, irrational opinions and attitudes toward members of diverse populations is considered to be ________.
Question 1 Select one:

A.
prejudice

B.
discrimination

C.
illegal

D.
immoral

E.
unethical
A. prejudice

An organization is a ________.
Question 2 Select one:

A.
collection of people working in different departments

B.
collection of people working together in a business

C.
collection of people working together to achieve a common purpose

D.
collection of people working on the same projects

E.
collection of people reporting to the same manager
C. collection of people working together to achieve a common purpose

An organization is a ________.
Question 2 Select one:

A.
collection of people working in different departments

B.
collection of people working together in a business

C.
collection of people working together to achieve a common purpose

D.
collection of people working on the same projects

E.
collection of people reporting to the same manager
C. collection of people working together to achieve a common purpose

A ________ is a person who is in charge of a small work group composed of non-managerial workers.
Question 3 Select one:

A.
line manager

B.
general manager

C.
functional manager

D.
middle manager

E.
team leader
E. team leader

A manager who is responsible for complex organizational units that include many functional areas of activity is ________.
Question 4 Select one:

A.
a staff manager

B.
a general manager

C.
a supervisor

D.
an administrator

E.
a team leader
B. a general manager

Shannon, a manager at Worth Industries and fluent in Mandarin, acts as a translator for the company's clients in China. Which of the following roles of a manager is Shannon performing in this scenario?
Question 5 Select one:

A.
disturbance handler

B.
monitor

C.
spokesperson

D.
resource allocator

E.
leader
C. spokesperson

In the context of the management process, ________ is the process of setting objectives and determining what should be done to accomplish them.
Question 6 Select one:

A.
organizing

B.
leading

C.
planning

D.
controlling

E.
inspiring
C. planning

Which of the following best describes lifelong learning?
Question 7 Select one:

A.
The ability to think analytically and achieve integrative problem solving.

B.
The ability to use expertise gained from past experiences to perform a task with proficiency.

C.
A change in behaviour that results from experience.

D.
The process of constantly learning from all experiences and opportunities.

E.
The process of measuring performance and taking action to ensure desired results.
D. The process of constantly learning from all experiences and opportunities.

Tristan is calculating the departments' inventory turnover statistics for the most recent quarter. Tristan is drawing mainly on ________ skills.
Question 8 Select one:

A.
supervisory

B.
conceptual

C.
strategic

D.
technical

E.
creative
D. technical

The ability to evaluate and examine information to make actual decisions and solve real problems is known as ________ competency.
Question 9 Select one:

A.
analytical

B.
innovation

C.
interpersonal

D.
technological

E.
information
A. analytical

One of the current trends in business intelligence is data visualization in the form of ________.
Question 10 Select one:

A.
executive dashboards

B.
data launching

C.
performance metrics

D.
intuitive dashboards

E.
executive panels
A. executive dashboards

Problem solving and decision making are dependent on quality ________ being available to the right people at the right time.
Question 11 Select one:

A.
information

B.
services

C.
products

D.
goals

E.
control
A. information

Managers who are regarded as problem solvers ________.
Question 12 Select one:

A.
will ignore information that would otherwise signal the presence of a performance threat

B.
actively process information and constantly look for problems to solve

C.
will anticipate performance threats and opportunities and take action to gain an advantage

D.
are unwillingly gather information, not wanting to make decisions or deal with problems

E.
will try to solve problems only when they are forced by the situation
C. will anticipate performance threats and opportunities and take action to gain an advantage

Managers who are regarded as problem solvers ________.
Question 12 Select one:

A.
will ignore information that would otherwise signal the presence of a performance threat

B.
actively process information and constantly look for problems to solve

C.
will anticipate performance threats and opportunities and take action to gain an advantage

D.
are unwillingly gather information, not wanting to make decisions or deal with problems

E.
will try to solve problems only when they are forced by the situation
B. actively process information and constantly look for problems to solve

Managers who are regarded as problem solvers ________.
Question 12 Select one:

A.
will ignore information that would otherwise signal the presence of a performance threat

B.
actively process information and constantly look for problems to solve

C.
will anticipate performance threats and opportunities and take action to gain an advantage

D.
are unwillingly gather information, not wanting to make decisions or deal with problems

E.
will try to solve problems only when they are forced by the situation
B. actively process information and constantly look for problems to solve

Cassidy is not keen on collecting facts and information while solving problems and is more spontaneous in decision making and usually chooses the first possible alternative to solve a problem. The type of decision being made in such situations is known as a ________ decision.
Question 13 Select one:

A.
absolute best

B.
conditional

C.
fully informed

D.
optimizing

E.
satisficing
E. satisficing

The ________ is particularly useful in describing how people make decisions under conditions of risk and uncertainty.
Question 14 Select one:

A.
descriptive decision model

B.
executive decision model

C.
behavioural decision model

D.
political decision model

E.
classical decision model
C. behavioural decision model

When people face complex environments and have limited information, they often use simplifying strategies for decision making. These simplifying strategies are known as ________.
Question 15 Select one:

A.
heuristics

B.
work simplification

C.
rationalization

D.
uncertainty reduction

E.
optimizing
A. heuristics

The ________ bias occurs when people assess the likelihood of something occurring based on its similarity to a stereotyped set of occurrences.
Question 16 Select one:

A.
representativeness

B.
availability

C.
escalation

D.
satisficing

E.
anchoring and adjustment
A. representativeness

Robin is planning to expand the family business internationally and is currently in the process of sourcing materials, manufacturing components, and locating business services around the world. Which of the following best describes Robin's actions?
Question 17 Select one:

A.
licensing

B.
insourcing

C.
global sourcing

D.
outsourcing

E.
franchising
C. global sourcing

McCain Foods Limited employs over 20,000 people at 57 facilities worldwide, generating over $6 billion in sales. These facilities worldwide are considered ________.
Question 18 Select one:

A.
regional offices

B.
global corporations

C.
regional economic alliances

D.
foreign subsidiaries

E.
national corporations
D. foreign subsidiaries

McCain Foods Limited employs over 20,000 people at 57 facilities worldwide, generating over $6 billion in sales. These facilities worldwide are considered ________.
Question 18 Select one:

A.
regional offices

B.
global corporations

C.
regional economic alliances

D.
foreign subsidiaries

E.
national corporations
D. foreign subsidiaries

McCain Foods Limited employs over 20,000 people at 57 facilities worldwide, generating over $6 billion in sales. These facilities worldwide are considered ________.
Question 18 Select one:

A.
regional offices

B.
global corporations

C.
regional economic alliances

D.
foreign subsidiaries

E.
national corporations
B. global corporations

In ________, the foreign firm buys the rights to use another's name and operating methods in its home country.
Question 19 Select one:

A.
global sourcing

B.
franchising

C.
exporting

D.
insourcing

E.
offshoring
B. franchising

Which of the following describes sweatshops?
Question 20 Select one:

A.
business operations that employ workers at low wages for long hours in poor working conditions

B.
local business operations that expand internationally through foreign direct investment

C.
arbitrating firms that settle labour disputes between employees and employers

D.
foreign firms that hire talented local personnel for training others in the host country

E.
foreign firms that are engaged in rampant corruption and bribery to gain easy access to the local resource pool
A. business operations that employ workers at low wages for long hours in poor working conditions

Which of the following is an example of child labour?
Question 21 Select one:

A.
a 13-year-old boy accompanying his mother to the carpet factory where she works

B.
a 19-year-old girl working as a salesgirl in the local supermarket

C.
a 13-year-old boy earning $10 for helping his father pick strawberries

D.
a 10-year-old boy working in a chemical factory

E.
a 10-year-old girl helping her younger brother with schoolwork
D. a 10-year-old boy working in a chemical factory

The Corruption of Foreign Public Officials Act (CFPOA) makes it illegal for ________.
Question 22 Select one:

A.
Canadian firms to invest overseas in businesses

B.
foreign firms to offer bribes to Canadian firms

C.
countries who are not members of the EU to invest in Canada

D.
Canadian firms and their representatives to offer bribes overseas

E.
foreign firms to offer nonmonetary gifts to Canadian officials
D. Canadian firms and their representatives to offer bribes overseas

Skyler Melville, a successful artist, lives and works in Paris. Skyler does not travel much and like many of Skyler's peers, has declined multiple offers to teach in famous schools of art outside of Paris. Skyler does not like change and prefers predictability, order, and structure in life. Skyler displays the traits associated with a ________ culture.
Question 23 Select one:

A.
long-term oriented

B.
short-term oriented

C.
low uncertainty avoidance

D.
xenocentric

E.
high uncertainty avoidance
E. high uncertainty avoidance

In a cross-cultural team, a member from the loose culture may show an inclination for which of the following?
Question 24 Select one:

A.
looking toward formal authority for direction

B.
being slow to volunteer

C.
being slow to display emotion

D.
always being on time

E.
criticizing others
C. being slow to display emotion

Ray is currently writing his doctoral thesis, which is a comparative study on the use and treatment of physical space in communication between people across cultures. His thesis is a study on ________.
Question 25 Select one:

A.
proxemics

B.
vocalics

C.
kinesics

D.
haptics

E.
chronemics
A. proxemics

One of the new employees at Aubrey's firm is from Asia and looks lost most of the time and is not interacting well with others in the office. Which of the following terms best describes this new employee's situation?
Question 26 Select one:

A.
culture shock

B.
ethnocentrism

C.
self-concordance

D.
proxemics

E.
ethical dilemma
A. culture shock

One of the concerns of multinational corporations is whether North American. management practices and theories are appropriate for use in foreign countries. Hofstede's research in this area indicates that North American theories ________.
Question 27 Select one:

A.
work best within English-speaking countries

B.
work best within American-based multinational corporations

C.
are only valid within the United States

D.
are still ethnocentric

E.
are universally applicable since they are good theories
D. are still ethnocentric

Lane, who was posted as a manager in a firm in India, was able to quickly adjust to the host country's culture and work ethics. Lane's self-confidence and flexible nature allowed Lane to accept and adapt to the cultural differences well. Which trait is displayed in the given scenario?
Question 28 Select one:

A.
xenocentrism

B.
intercultural competency

C.
ethnocentrism

D.
egocentrism

E.
uncertainty avoidance
B. intercultural competency

Max Weber was concerned that people in nineteenth-century organizations were in positions of authority due to their ________ rather than their ________.
Question 29 Select one:

A.
managerial competence; economic wealth

B.
political connections; leadership traits

C.
leadership qualities; job requirements

D.
economic wealth; social standing

E.
social standing; job-related capabilities
D. economic wealth; social standing

Max Weber was concerned that people in nineteenth-century organizations were in positions of authority due to their ________ rather than their ________.
Question 29 Select one:

A.
managerial competence; economic wealth

B.
political connections; leadership traits

C.
leadership qualities; job requirements

D.
economic wealth; social standing

E.
social standing; job-related capabilities
E. social standing; job-related capabilities

Which of the following best states the impetus for the development of a bureaucratic organization?
Question 30 Select one:

A.
Max Weber was interested in formulating exact rules of behaviour for German managers.

B.
Max Weber was attempting to identify a common set of employee needs in German society.

C.
Max Weber was reacting to performance deficiencies in organizations of his day.

D.
Max Weber was attempting to upset German society.

E.
Max Weber was trying to define the one best way to perform a job.
C. Max Weber was reacting to performance deficiencies in organizations of his day.
